beats.activity: Wake up with Max, coffee and morning routine at home.
beats.reasoning: Standard wake-up beat; Isaac starts his day at home in Fremont with his dog before a shift day. Moderate energy from yesterday (55/100) means a short, unhurried morning.

beats.activity: Walk Max through the neighborhood, grab oat milk latte at local café.
beats.reasoning: Isaac's routine ritual with Max; mild partly-cloudy weather is perfect for a morning walk. Stays local in Fremont before heading to the station.

beats.activity: Commute to Station 41 via car (transit strike in effect).
beats.reasoning: Metro buses and Link suspended until 4pm; Isaac drives instead of his usual transit route. Extra 15 minutes budgeted for the U-District commute without light rail.

beats.activity: Station duties: equipment checks, morning drills, respond to calls.
beats.reasoning: 24-hour rotation shift; typical morning block of drills and early calls. Partly cloudy mild weather keeps call volume moderate.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a hydrating treat at Molly Moon's.
beats.reasoning: Routine lunch spot within walking distance of the station; Isaac values the ritual and the cool, refreshing break during a shift day.

beats.activity: Afternoon shift: respond to calls, station maintenance, crew time.
beats.reasoning: Second half of 24-hour shift; mild weather keeps call volume steady but not intense. Isaac is unbothered and paces himself.

beats.activity: Dinner with crew at the station: simple meal and downtime.
beats.reasoning: Crew meal during shift; Isaac uses this time to decompress and talk shop with coworkers before heading home after 24 hours.

beats.activity: Evening calls and station duties wind down.
beats.reasoning: Final hours of 24-hour shift; mostly administrative tasks and prep for night crew handoff. Mild evening keeps things calm.

beats.activity: Drive home from station; transit strike still in effect until 4pm (now lifted), but Isaac sticks with car for consistency.
beats.reasoning: End of 24-hour rotation; Isaac heads home to Fremont. Transit strike ended at 4pm but he's already committed to driving; typical U-District to Fremont commute ~35–40 min.

beats.activity: Evening at home: greet Max, light dinner, review photography or potter at wheel.
beats.reasoning: Post-shift wind-down; Isaac is laid-back and values quiet time with Max. Mild weather and moderate energy mean he stays home, maybe works on a film project or clay.

world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
